layers: The chart displays four line series representing different fossil fuel and industrial emission sources from 1880 to 2020. Coal (top line, gray) reaches the highest values near 14 000 million metric tonnes. Oil (second dashed line, light gray) reaches approximately 10 000–11 000. Gas (third dashed line, light gray) reaches approximately 7 000–8 000. Cement, Flaring & Other Industry (bottom line, highlighted in blue/teal) is the lowest series, approximately 2 000 by 2020. The Cement, Flaring & Other Industry series is visually highlighted with a blue tooltip callout showing the 2009 value of 1 799. The other three series appear faded/gray in comparison.
filters: The 'Cement, Flaring & Other Industry' series is selected/highlighted with a tooltip annotation for year 2009 showing value 1 799. The other series (Coal, Oil, Gas) are visually de-emphasized in gray.
aria_label: This line chart shows global CO2 emissions by fuel type and sector of activity from 1880 to 2020, measured in millions of metric tonnes. Four series are displayed: Coal (highest, reaching approximately 14 000, filtered out), Oil (approximately 10 000–11 000, filtered out), Gas (approximately 7 000–8 000, filtered out), and Cement, Flaring & Other Industry (lowest, highlighted, showing 1 799 in 2009 and approximately 2 000 by 2020).

layers: Two dominant point layers: pink/rose dots representing 'Gauche' (left political bloc) and dark blue dots representing 'Droite' (right political bloc), both distributed across the full range of the chart. Faded lighter dots for 'Extrême gauche', 'Centre', 'Divers', and 'Extrême droite' are present but visually de-emphasized. Points are densely clustered between 1 500 and 4 000 €/m² on the x-axis and between 30% and 55% abstention on the y-axis.
aria_label: This scatter plot shows the relationship between median property prices per square metre (in euros, on a logarithmic x-axis ranging from 1 000 to 10 000 €/m²) and the second-round abstention rate (%) for various French constituencies, colour-coded by political bloc. Pink dots representing 'Gauche', and dark blue dots representing 'Droite' are highlighted with selected filters in the legend on the right, with other blocs Extrême gauche, Centre, Divers and Extrême droite filtered out.

layers: The map shows Corsica divided into communes with a diverging color scale ranging from light pink/beige (low values near 0) to dark blue (high values near 47). Ajaccio is highlighted in dark blue indicating the highest concentration of electric chargers. Bastia appears in a medium blue. Porto-Vecchio shows a medium purple-blue tone. Most inland and rural communes appear in light gray or faint pink, indicating few or no electric chargers. A mountainous region in the center-east is annotated as 'Region montagneuse' and appears largely gray.
filters: No dropdown filters visible. Municipality labels are displayed for key towns: Ajaccio, Bastia, Calvi, Algajola, Vico, Corte, Alando, Cervione, Altiani, Azzana, Antisanti, Aléria, Ghisonaccia, Afa, Albitreccia, Olmeto, Propriano, Sartène, Zonsa, Porto-Vecchio, Bonifacio.
aria_label: This choropleth map titled 'Données Chargeurs Électrique' shows the distribution of electric vehicle chargers across communes in Corsica, France. The color scale ranges from 0 (light pink) to 47 (dark blue), indicating the number of chargers per commune and mid-range being purple for Porto-Vecchio. Ajaccio, the island's capital on the west coast, has the highest number of chargers shown in dark blue, while Bastia in the north also shows notable concentration. The majority of inland and mountainous communes appear in light gray and have 0 chargers.

layers: Each bar is stacked with four color bands representing age groups: dark navy blue at the bottom for 18-29, medium blue for 30-49, light blue for 50-64, and very light blue/grey at the top for 65+. The 18-29 segment is the most visually prominent as it is labeled with percentage values in white text inside the dark bottom segment.
aria_label: This stacked bar chart shows the percentage of U.S. adults who use selected social networks, broken down by age group, based on a survey conducted February 1 through June 10, 2024. Each vertical bar on the X-axis represents a social media platform — Facebook (24.7%), Instagram (38.6%), X (44.2%), Pinterest (30.5%), Snapchat (56.5%), YouTube (27.5%), Reddit (47.9%), and TikTok (43.7%) — stacked by four age groups: 18-29, 30-49, 50-64, and 65+. The Y-axis goes from 0 to 100% in steps of 20. The 18-29 age group is highlighted while the other age groups are faded. The 18-29 age group (dark navy segment, highlighted in the chart) shows the highest usage share on Snapchat at 56.5%, followed by Reddit at 47.9% and TikTok at 43.7%, while Facebook has the lowest share among young adults at 24.7%.

layers: Six box plots displayed on a logarithmic Y-axis, one per political bloc (Extrême gauche, Gauche, Centre, Divers, Droite, Extrême droite). The Gauche box is highlighted in pink/red with active tooltips showing all five-number summary values plus fences. Centre box is yellow. Droite box is blue. Extrême droite and Divers boxes are light grey. Extrême gauche box is light salmon/pink with a red outline. Individual outlier points are shown above and below whiskers for several blocs, with notable city labels annotated.
aria_label: This is a box plot using DVF 2024 data showing the median price per square metre (in euros, on a logarithmic scale) of real estate transactions grouped by winning political bloc (Bloc politique vainqueur). The six blocs shown on the X-axis are Extrême gauche, Gauche, Centre, Divers, Droite, and Extrême droite. For the Gauche bloc, the median price is 2 571 €/m², with a Q1 of 1 801, Q3 of 3 452, and a maximum outlier of 9 500 (Paris); the minimum is 833 (Revin). The Droite bloc appears to have the highest typical prices, while Extrême droite and Divers show lower medians.

layers: The map shows Corsica divided into communes, colored on a gradient scale from light gray/white (0 chargers) to dark teal/blue (47 chargers). Ajaccio appears in the darkest blue, indicating the highest concentration of electric chargers. Porto-Vecchio is highlighted in medium teal with an active tooltip showing a value of 14. Bastia also shows a notable teal color. Corte and a few other communes show light green tints. The mountainous interior region is labeled 'Region montagneuse' and appears predominantly gray, indicating few or no chargers.
aria_label: This choropleth map displays data on electric vehicle chargers ('Données Chargeurs Électrique') across communes of Corsica, France, with values ranging from 0 to 47. Ajaccio, the island's capital, appears in the darkest blue, and has the highest number of chargers. Porto-Vecchio is currently selected with a tooltip showing 14 chargers, and Bastia also shows a notable concentration. The mountainous interior of the island has very few or no chargers, shown in gray.

layers: The map of Corsica is divided into municipalities colored on a gradient scale from light gray/white (0 chargers) to dark navy blue (up to 47 chargers). Ajaccio is highlighted in dark navy blue indicating the highest concentration of electric chargers. Bastia is highlighted in dark teal/blue with a tooltip showing 38 chargers. Several municipalities including Corte, Porto-Vecchio, Sartène, and Zonza show light green tints indicating moderate values. Most of the island's interior is in gray indicating zero or very few chargers.
filters: A tooltip is active on Bastia showing the value 38. The legend shows a continuous color scale from 0 to 47.
aria_label: This choropleth map titled 'Données Chargeurs Électrique' shows the distribution of electric vehicle chargers across municipalities in Corsica, France. The color scale ranges from 0 to 47 chargers, with darker blue indicating higher counts. Ajaccio has the highest number of chargers, while Bastia shows 38 chargers as indicated by an active tooltip. Most of the island's interior municipalities have very few or no electric chargers.

layers: Each bar is stacked with four age group segments: 18-29 (dark blue, bottom), 30-49 (medium blue), 50-64 (light gray/blue), and 65+ (lightest gray, top). YouTube has the tallest overall bar, exceeding 300%. Facebook and Instagram bars reach approximately 270%. X, Pinterest, Snapchat, Reddit, and TikTok have shorter bars. Snapchat has a small pink/magenta highlight visible at the base, possibly indicating a selected or notable segment.
filters: Legend pills for four age groups: 18-29, 30-49, 50-64, 65+. No dropdown filters visible.
aria_label: This stacked bar chart shows the percentage of U.S. adults who say they 'ever use' selected social networks, broken down by age group (18-29, 30-49, 50-64, 65+), based on a survey conducted February 1 to June 10, 2024 by Pew Research. YouTube has the highest combined usage, followed by Facebook and Instagram. X, Pinterest, Snapchat, and Reddit show lower totals. Platforms like X, Pinterest, Snapchat, and Reddit show lower overall usage totals. The 18-29 age group (dark blue) tends to form the largest single segment for platforms like Snapchat and TikTok, while Facebook shows relatively stronger representation from older age groups.

layers: Seven stacked area series representing Facebook, Instagram, X, Pinterest, Snapchat, YouTube, Reddit, and TikTok. The combined stack peaks near 420-430 for the 18-29 age group and declines steadily to approximately 170 for the 65+ age group. Each platform is rendered in a distinct shade of tan, beige, olive, or dark brown. The bottom layers (Facebook and YouTube) remain relatively thick across all age groups, while upper layers (Snapchat, TikTok, Instagram) taper significantly for older age groups.
aria_label: This stacked area chart shows the percentage of U.S. adults who say they 'ever use' selected social networks — Facebook, Instagram, X, Pinterest, Snapchat, YouTube, Reddit, and TikTok — broken down by four age groups: 18-29, 30-49, 50-64, and 65+. The combined usage across all platforms is highest for the 18-29 age group, and declines sharply with age for adults aged 65 and older. The main trend is that social media usage — especially platforms like Snapchat, TikTok, and Instagram — decreases significantly among older age groups, while Facebook and YouTube retain relatively more usage across all ages. Data is from a Pew Research survey conducted February 1–June 10, 2024.

layers: Each bar is divided into four stacked color segments representing age groups: darkest navy blue for 18-29 (bottom), medium dark blue for 30-49, medium blue for 50-64, and lightest blue for 65+ (top). All bars reach approximately 100%. The 18-29 segment appears largest for Snapchat and TikTok, while Facebook and YouTube show more even distribution across age groups.

filters.label: Platforms shown
filters.value: Facebook, Instagram, X, Pinterest, Snapchat, YouTube, Reddit, TikTok

aria_label: Stacked bar chart titled 'Social media demographics' showing the percentage of U.S. adults who say they ever use selected social networks, broken down by four age groups (18-29, 30-49, 50-64, and 65+). Each bar represents a platform — Facebook, Instagram, X, Pinterest, Snapchat, YouTube, Reddit, and TikTok — and all bars reach 100% cumulatively. Younger adults (18-29) dominate usage on Snapchat and TikTok, while platforms like Facebook and YouTube show broader usage across all age groups. Data is from a Pew Research survey conducted February 1 through June 10, 2024.

layers: Each bar is stacked with four age group segments colored in progressively lighter shades of blue from bottom (18-29, darkest) to top (65+, lightest). Snapchat's bar has a highlighted magenta/pink segment for the 18-29 age group, drawing attention to that platform's younger skew. YouTube has the tallest bar, reaching approximately 300%, indicating high usage across all age groups.
aria_label: Stacked bar chart titled 'Social media demographics' showing the percentage of U.S. adults who say they ever use selected social networks, broken down by four age groups: 18-29, 30-49, 50-64, and 65+. The 18-29 group is highlighted on the chart, while the others are faded in background. YouTube has the highest combined usage, and Facebook is second while X (Twitter) and Reddit show comparatively lower totals. The data is from a Pew Research survey conducted February 1 through June 10, 2024.

layers: The chart shows multiple stacked area layers representing different social media platforms. YouTube (highlighted in bright red) is the selected/active series, shown prominently in the middle of the stack. Other platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X, Pinterest, Snapchat, Reddit, TikTok) appear in muted gray and dark tones. A vertical selection line is drawn at the 50-64 age group, with a tooltip showing YouTube's value of 86. The overall stack is tallest at age 18-29 and decreases toward the 65+ group.
aria_label: This stacked area chart shows the percentage of U.S. adults who say they 'ever use' selected social networks, broken down by age group (18-29, 30-49, 50-64, and 65+). YouTube is highlighted in red and is currently selected, showing a value of 86% for the 50-64 age group. The overall combined usage across all platforms is highest among younger adults (18-29) and declines with age, with the total stacked values being highest for the youngest group and dropping notably for the 65+ group. The data comes from a Pew Research survey of U.S. adults conducted February 1–June 10, 2024.

layers: Each bar is stacked with four color bands representing age groups: 18-29 (lightest gray, bottom), 30-49 (medium gray), 50-64 (dark blue, highlighted with percentage labels), and 65+ (light blue, top). The 50-64 segment is distinctly highlighted in dark blue across all platforms.
aria_label: Stacked bar chart titled 'Données démographiques relatives aux réseaux sociaux' showing the percentage of American adults who say they 'sometimes use' certain social networks, broken down by age group. The chart covers eight platforms: Facebook, Instagram, X, Pinterest, Snapchat, YouTube, Reddit, and TikTok. The 50-64 age segment is highlighted in dark blue, and the others faded, with values showing Facebook (25,5%), Instagram (18,3%), X (17,4%), Pinterest (23,4%), Snapchat (12,2%), Youtube (25,4%), Reddit (11,5%) and TikTok (19,3%). YouTube and Facebook show the highest share of 50-64 users, while Snapchat and Reddit have the lowest shares of that age group.

layers: The map of Corsica is divided into communes shaded from light gray (low values near 0) to dark/black (high values near 47). Ajaccio is the darkest region, indicating the highest concentration of electric chargers. Porto-Vecchio and Zonza appear in medium-dark gray. A large central mountainous region ('Region montagneuse') is labeled and appears in light to medium gray. The majority of communes are rendered in light gray, indicating low or zero chargers.
aria_label: This choropleth map titled 'Données Chargeurs Électrique' shows the distribution of electric vehicle chargers across the communes of Corsica, France. The color scale ranges from 0 (light gray) to 47 (dark/black). Ajaccio stands out as the darkest commune, indicating the highest number of electric chargers, while Porto-Vecchio shows moderate levels. The majority of rural and mountainous communes across the island have very few or no chargers.

layers: Four distinct lines are displayed on a dark background. Coal (dashed line) reaches the highest values near 14 000 million metric tonnes by 2021. Oil (dashed line) reaches approximately 12 000. Gas (solid line) reaches approximately 7 000-8 000. Cement, Flaring & Other Industry (solid line, lightest) remains the lowest, reaching approximately 2 000. All lines show near-flat trends until roughly 1950, then sharp upward trajectories.
filters: No interactive filters visible; series are distinguished by line style (dashed vs solid) and labeled directly on the chart.
aria_label: This line chart shows global CO2 emissions by fuel type and activity sector from 1880 to 2020, measured in millions of metric tonnes. Four series are represented: Coal, Oil, Gas, and Cement, Flaring & Other Industry. No series is filtered or highlighted. All fuel types show relatively flat emissions until around 1950, after which rapid and sustained growth is observed in all categories, with coal being the largest single source of CO2 emissions by 2020.

layers: Each small multiple contains a single dark teal/navy line chart on a white background showing house price trends over time. All panels share the same Y-axis scale (0 to £400 000) and X-axis range (approximately 1995 to 2023). London stands out visually with prices reaching near or above £400 000, while regions like North East, Northern Ireland, Scotland, Wales, and Yorkshire remain well below £200 000. Northern Ireland shows a distinctive spike and sharp drop around 2007-2008 before recovering gradually.
filters: No interactive filters or legend pills visible. The dashboard displays all 13 regions/nations simultaneously: East Midlands, East of England, England, London, North East, North West, Northern Ireland, Scotland, South East, South West, United Kingdom, Wales, West Midlands Region, Yorkshire and The Humber.
aria_label: This is a small multiples line chart titled 'House prices in the U.K.' showing average prices of residential properties across East Midlands, East of England, England, London, North East, North West, Northern Ireland, Scotland, South East, South West, United Kingdom, Wales, West Midlands Region, Yorkshire and The Humber from 2000 to 2023. The Y-axis for all panels ranges from £0 to £400 000. No interactive filters or legend pills visible. London shows the highest house prices, approaching and exceeding £400 000 by 2023, while regions such as the North East and Yorkshire and The Humber remain below £200 000 throughout the period. Northern Ireland exhibits a notable spike around 2007 followed by a sharp decline making it the most distinctive trend among all regions.

layers: Multiple stacked area series representing different fuel types and sectors
Coal layer appears at the top in dark gray, reaching the highest values (~14 000) by 2021
Oil layer below Coal in medium gray, reaching approximately 10 000–11 000 range
Gas layer below Oil, reaching approximately 7 000–8 000 range
Cement, Flaring & Other Industry layer at the bottom in light gray/white, smallest contributor

Annotation callout pointing to Cement, Flaring & Other Industry series showing year 2000 value of 1 217
filters: Legend labels visible on right side: Coal, Oil, Gas, Cement Flaring & Other Industry
aria_label: This stacked area chart shows global CO2 emissions by fuel type and activity sector from 1880 to 2020, measured in millions of metric tonnes. The chart includes four layers: Coal, Oil, Gas, and Cement, Flaring & Other Industry. The Cement, Flaring and Other Industry line is selected and the others are faded. There is a tooltip on the Cement line for the year 2000 showing a value of 1 217.

layers: Each bar is divided into four stacked color segments representing age groups: 18-29 (darkest blue, bottom), 30-49 (medium blue), 50-64 (light blue/grey), and 65+ (lightest, top). The 18-29 segment is highlighted with explicit percentage labels displayed in white text within each bar. The upper segments are progressively lighter in color.
aria_label: Stacked bar chart titled 'Données démographiques relatives aux réseaux sociaux' showing the percentage of American adults who say they 'sometimes use' certain social networks, broken down by age group:  18-29, 30-49, 50-64, and 65+ The chart covers percentage share on eight platforms on the X-axis: Facebook (24,7%), Instagram (38,6%), X (44,2%), Pinterest (30,5%), Snapchat (56,5%), YouTube (27,5%), Reddit (47,9%), and TikTok (43,7%). The 18-29 age group is highlighted and the other age groups are faded. Snapchat has the highest share of 18-29 year old users at 56,5%, while Facebook has the lowest at 24,7%.

layers: Each bar is divided into five stacked segments representing age groups: 18-29 (dark blue, highlighted with percentage labels), 30-49 (medium blue), 50-64 (light blue/gray), and 65+ (lightest gray). The 18-29 segment is prominently displayed in dark navy blue with white percentage labels, drawing visual emphasis. The older age segments are rendered in progressively lighter shades.
aria_label: This stacked bar chart shows the percentage of U.S. adults who say they ever social media platforms, broken down by age group, based on a Pew Research survey conducted February 1 to June 10, 2024. The eight platforms shown on the X-axis are Facebook (28.4%), Instagram (33.5%), X (29.1%), Pinterest (30.5%), Snapchat (27.8%), YouTube (27.8%), Reddit (36.5%), and TikTok (29.6%), with the 30-49 age group highlighted in dark blue, the rest of the age groups are faded. Reddit shows the highest share of 30-49 year-old users at 36.5%, followed by Instagram at 33.5%, while Facebook has the lowest share of that age group at 28.4%.

layers: Choropleth fill layer showing winning political bloc per arrondissement: pink/rose for Gauche (left), blue for Droite (right), yellow for Centre
Proportional circle overlay on each arrondissement representing median price per m² — smaller circles indicate more affordable areas, larger circles indicate more expensive areas
Circle colors match the bloc vainqueur: pink circles on Gauche arrondissements, blue circles on Droite arrondissements, yellow circle on Centre arrondissement
Highlighted info panel on the right showing Gauche as the overall winner

filters.label: legend
filters.value: Prix médian au m²: Plus abordable (small circle), Plus cher (large circle)

aria_label: This is a choropleth map of Paris arrondissements showing the winning political bloc (Gauche, Centre, or Droite) in each district, overlaid with proportional circles representing median property prices per square metre. The overall winner is Gauche (left bloc), winning 11 out of 17 arrondissements, shown in pink across most of central, eastern, and southern Paris. Droite (right bloc) won several western arrondissements shown in blue, while Centre won one arrondissement shown in yellow in central Paris. Circle size indicates the median property price and larger circles indicate higher median property prices, with the most expensive areas appearing in central and western arrondissements.

layers: Each bar is divided into four stacked color segments representing age groups: 18-29 (light gray, bottom), 30-49 (dark blue, highlighted with percentage labels), 50-64 (medium gray), and 65+ (lightest blue/gray, top). The 30-49 segment is consistently highlighted in dark navy blue across all platforms.
aria_label: This stacked bar chart shows the percentage of U.S. adults who say they ever use selected social media platforms, broken down by age group, based on a Pew Research survey conducted February 1 to June 10, 2024. The eight platforms shown on the X-axis are Facebook (28,4%), Instagram (33,5%), X (29,1%), Pinterest (30,5%), Snapchat (27,8%), YouTube (27,8%), Reddit (36,5%), and TikTok (29,6%), with the 30-49 age group highlighted in dark blue, the rest of the age groups are faded. Reddit shows the highest share of 30-49 year-old users at 36,5%, followed by Instagram at 33,5%, while Facebook has the lowest share of this age group at 28,4%.

layers: Four lines are displayed: Coal (solid light blue/cyan line) is the highest series reaching approximately 14 000 by 2021; Oil (dashed medium blue line) reaches approximately 11 000; Gas (dotted dark blue/navy line) reaches approximately 7 500; Cement, Flaring & Other Industry (solid dark navy line) is the lowest series reaching approximately 2 000. All lines start near zero around 1850-1880 and show exponential growth, particularly after 1950.

Four series are tracked: Coal reaches the highest level, followed by Oil, Gas, and Cement, Flaring & Other Industry. No tooltip, no series is highlighted or filtered.

layers: The chart contains stacked area bands for 7 social networks: Facebook (bottom, warm muted red/brown), Instagram, X, Pinterest, Snapchat, YouTube, and Reddit/TikTok layered above. aria_label: This choropleth map with proportional circles shows the election results by arrondissement in Marseille, France. The winner is Gauche (left bloc), which won 12 out of 16 arrondissements, shown in pink, while Extrême droite (far-right) won the remaining arrondissements shown in dark grey, mostly in the southern and eastern parts of the city. Overlaid grey circles of varying sizes indicate the median property price per square metre in each arrondissement, with larger circles representing more expensive areas. The left-leaning arrondissements are concentrated in the northern and coastal western parts of Marseille, while far-right victories are clustered in the southern inland districts. No tooltip is visible, and nothing is highlighted.

layers: The map shows Corsica divided into communes colored on a gradient from light gray/white (0 chargers) to dark navy blue (47 chargers). Ajaccio on the west coast is the darkest blue, indicating the highest count. Bastia in the north, Corte in the center, Porto-Vecchio in the south-east, Zonza, and Sartène show medium teal-green shades indicating moderate charger counts. The majority of the island's communes are light gray indicating few or no chargers. A tooltip popup is displayed over Ajaccio showing the value 47. An annotation arrow points to a mountainous region in the northeast labeled 'Region montagneuse'.
aria_label: This choropleth map titled 'Données Chargeurs Électrique' (Electric Charger Data) shows the distribution of electric vehicle chargers across communes in Corsica, France. The color scale ranges from 0 to 47 chargers, with Ajaccio having the highest count of 47 chargers shown in dark navy blue. A tooltip over Ajaccio is visible, showing 47. Other notable communes with chargers include Bastia, Corte, Porto-Vecchio, Zonza, and Sartène shown in teal-green, while the majority of communes, particularly the mountainous interior region, have few or no chargers and appear in light gray.

layers.position: far left, very small sliver visible in some rows
fourth segment, largest in expensive communes, approximately 60–95% range

aria_label: This horizontal stacked bar chart, shows the political breakdown of French municipalities according to their real estate prices per square meter, divided into five categories ranging from the least expensive (€821 - €1 844/m²) to the most expensive (€3 863 -€10 276/m²). Each bar represents 100% of the municipalities in a price category, subdivided into segments according to political orientation (Extrême gauche, Gauche, Centre, Divers, Droite, Extrême Droite). There is a year filter on the DVF Data, with 2025 selected. No tooltip is visible, no segment is highlighted.

layers: Each bar is divided into four stacked color segments representing age groups: darkest navy (18-29) at the bottom, dark blue (30-49), medium blue (50-64), and light blue (65+) at the top. YouTube has the tallest bar reaching above 300%, followed by Facebook near 270% and Instagram near 200%. X and Reddit have the shortest bars. The segments vary in proportion across platforms, with older age groups (65+) notably larger for Facebook and YouTube.
aria_label: This stacked bar chart titled 'Social media demographics' shows the percentage of U.S. adults who say they ever use selected social networks, broken down by four age groups: 18-29, 30-49, 50-64, and 65+. The X-axis shows the platforms: Facebook, Instagram, X, Pinterest, Snapchat, YouTube, Reddit and TikTok. No tooltip is visible, no bar or segment is highlighted.

layers: Five horizontal stacked bars, each representing a price category of communes. Each bar is divided into six colored segments: Extrême gauche (dark red), Gauche (pink/rose), Centre (yellow), Divers (grey), Droite (blue), Extrême droite (dark navy/black). The most expensive communes (Les plus chères) show a dominant blue (Droite) segment and a large pink (Gauche) segment with no visible Extrême gauche. The least expensive communes (Les moins chères) show a notable dark navy (Extrême droite) segment and a small dark red (Extrême gauche) segment. Middle categories show a mix of all parties.
aria_label: This stacked bar chart shows the political orientation of French communes grouped by real estate price categories, measured as the share of communes (%) on the X-axis. The five price categories range from 'Les plus chères' (3 864–10 959 €/m²) at the top to 'Les moins chères' (833–1 807 €/m²) at the bottom. A legend at the bottom is visible to denote the different political blocs with colored squares: Dark Red - Extrême gauche, Pink - Gauche, Yellow - Centre, Gray - Divers, Blue - Droite and Black - Extrême droite. No tooltip is visible, no segment is selected or highlighted.

layers: Each dot represents a locality colored by political bloc: dark red for Extrême gauche, pink/rose for Gauche, yellow for Centre, gray for Divers, blue for Droite, and black for Extrême droite. Points are scattered across the full range of both axes with no single color dominating any particular region, though high abstention outliers (above 60%) appear mostly in the 2 000–3 000 €/m² range.
aria_label: This scatter plot shows the relationship between median property price per square metre (in euros, on a logarithmic X-axis from 1 000 to 10 000) and the abstention rate at the second round of an election (Y-axis from 0% to 70%), with each point colored by political bloc (Extrême gauche, Gauche, Centre, Divers, Droite, Extrême droite). On the right is a legend listing all the blocs. No filter is selected and no tooltip is visible.

layers: The chart uses stacked area layers representing multiple social media platforms (Facebook, Instagram, X, Pinterest, Snapchat, YouTube, Reddit, TikTok). The Reddit layer is visually highlighted in a golden/tan color and is actively selected, while all other platform layers appear in darker muted gray tones, faded into the background. A tooltip is displayed at the 50-64 age group for Reddit showing a value of 11.
aria_label: This is a stacked area chart titled 'Social media demographics' showing the percentage of U.S. adults who say they 'ever use' selected social networks, broken down by age group (18-29, 30-49, 50-64, 65+). Layers are Facebook, Instagram, X, Pinterest, Snapchat, YouTube, Reddit and TikTok, also listed in a legend at the top of the chart. The Reddit layer is highlighted, with a tooltip showing that 11% of adults aged 50-64 use Reddit. Data is from a Pew Research survey conducted February 1–June 10, 2024.

Points are color-coded by political bloc: pink/red for Gauche, grey for Divers, light blue for Droite, lighter shades for Extrême gauche and Extrême droite. The X-axis uses a logarithmic scale.

aria_label: This scatter plot shows the relationship between median property price per square metre (X-axis, log scale, in euros) and the abstention rate at the second round of an election (Y-axis, in percent) for French communes. Four points are selected: Revin with tooltip showing (833 €/m², 44,2% abstention, Gauche), Nîmes with tooltip showing (2 469 €/m², 42,1% abstention, Gauche), Sartène with tooltip showing (2 792 €/m², 9,0% abstention, Divers), Paris with tooltip showing (9 500 €/m², 38,4% abstention, Gauche). The rest of the points are faded in the background. On the right, in the filter legend, the Gauche and Divers filters are selected. The other filters - Extrême gauche, Centre, Droite and Extrême droite are not selected.

layers: Each small multiple contains a single dark teal/navy line chart showing price growth over time from approximately 2000 to 2023. London is visually notable for its steep rise and higher peak value reaching near £500k. A tooltip annotation is visible on each chart indicating a recent or peak price value. The London chart includes a specific date annotation 'mai 2016' near the £467k marker. All charts share the same Y-axis scale (0 to £400 000) enabling cross-region comparison.
filters: No interactive filters visible; the dashboard displays all 14 regions simultaneously as a fixed small multiples layout. Regions include: East Midlands, East of England, England, London, North East, North West, Northern Ireland, Scotland, South East, South West, United Kingdom, Wales, West Midlands Region, Yorkshire and The Humber.
aria_label: Small multiples line chart titled 'House prices in the U.K.' showing average residential property prices in regions across the U.K. from 2000 to 2023. Each region has a tooltip point displaying a value: East Midlands - £169k, East of England - £264k, England - £226k, London - showing mai 2016 £467k, North East - £125k, North West - £148k, Northern Ireland - £123k, Scotland - £138k, South East - £301k, South West - £231k, United Kingdom - £211k, Wales - £144k, West Midlands Region - £175k, Yorkshire and The Humber - £149k.

layers.highlighted_series: Gauche (pink/red dots) are rendered in full opacity and are visually dominant
layers.faded_series: Extrême gauche, Centre, Divers, Droite, Extrême droite are rendered in faded/muted pastel tones
layers.color_encoding: Each political bloc is encoded by a distinct color: pink for Extrême gauche, red/pink for Gauche, yellow for Centre, light gray for Divers, light blue for Droite, medium gray for Extrême droite

aria_label: This scatter plot examines the relationship between median property prices per square metre (X-axis, logarithmic scale from 1 000 to 10 000 €/m²) and the second-round abstention rate (Y-axis, 0% to 70%) across French constituencies, colour-coded by political bloc. The plot shows the 'Gauche' (left) bloc is highlighted in red-pink and the other points are filtered out. In the legend to the right, Gauche is selected and the rest - Extrême gauche, Centre, Divers, Droite and Extrême droite - are not selected.

layers: Five series lines are displayed. The Coal series (cyan/teal line) is highlighted and selected, showing a tooltip for year 1986 with value 8 336. The other four series — Oil (light gray), Gas (gray dashed), Cement/Flaring & Other Industry (light gray), and an additional series — are faded/muted in the background. A pink/magenta highlighted band appears around the 1930s–1940s period on the Coal line. The Coal line rises sharply from the 1950s onward, reaching approximately 14 000 by 2021.
filters: The 'Coal' series is selected/highlighted. Other series (Oil, Gas, Cement, Flaring & Other Industry) are visually de-emphasized. The tooltip is active on the Coal line at year 1986.
aria_label: This line chart shows global CO2 emissions by fuel type and activity sector from 1880 to 2020, measured in millions of metric tonnes. Four series are shown: Coal, Oil, Gas, Cement/Flaring & Other Industry. The Coal series is highlighted, with a tooltip showing the year 1986 and 8 336. The other series are shown in the background.

layers: Each region is displayed as a separate small multiple panel arranged in a 4-column grid. Lines are drawn in dark teal/navy blue. Some series use dotted lines (East Midlands, South West, West Midlands Region) likely indicating estimated or modeled data, while others use solid lines indicating official data. Northern Ireland shows a distinct spike and dip around 2007-2008 before recovering. London shows the steepest upward trajectory of all regions.

filters.label: Regions shown
filters.value: East Midlands, East of England, England, London, North East, North West, Northern Ireland, Scotland, South East, South West, United Kingdom, Wales, West Midlands Region, Yorkshire and The Humber

aria_label: This small multiples line chart shows average residential house prices across the U.K.'s regions: East Midlands, East of England, England, London, North East, North West, Northern Ireland, Scotland, South East, South West, United Kingdom, Wales, West Midlands Region, Yorkshire and The Humber. On the X-axis we see 2000 to 2023 and on the Y-axis 0, 200 000, and £400 000. No tooltip is visible and nothing is highlighted.

layers: The map of Corsica uses a color gradient from light gray (zero or no data) through light green to dark blue to represent the number of electric chargers per municipality. The majority of municipalities are shown in gray, indicating low or no chargers. Ajaccio is highlighted in dark blue (highest concentration), Bastia in medium blue, and Porto-Vecchio, Corte, Sartène, Zonfa, and Aléria in light green shades. A label 'Region montagneuse' with an arrow points to the mountainous interior region, which remains gray.
aria_label: This choropleth map of Corsica shows data on electric vehicle chargers by municipality, titled 'Données Chargeurs Électrique'. The color scale ranges from 0 to 47 chargers, with gray indicating zero or very few chargers and dark blue indicating the highest number. Ajaccio stands out with the darkest blue color, with the highest concentration of chargers and Bastia is second darkest; Porto-Vecchio, Corte, Bonifacio, and Sartène are colored different shades from green to lighter green. A map annotation labels the montainous interior as Region Montagneuse.

layers: Each bar is divided into four stacked segments representing age groups: 18-29 (darkest gray, bottom), 30-49 (medium gray), 50-64 (light gray), and 65+ (light blue, top). The top segment (65+) is highlighted in blue for each platform and labeled with its percentage value. The bars all reach approximately 100%.
aria_label: This stacked bar chart shows the percentage of American adults who report 'sometimes using' certain social media platforms, broken down by age group (18-29, 30-49, 50-64, and 65+). The segment representing adults aged 65+, is highlighted in blue and labeled for each platform: Facebook at 21,5%, Instagram at 9,6%, X at 9,3%, Pinterest at 15,6%, Snapchat at 3,5%, YouTube at 19,2%, Reddit at 4,2% and TikTok at 7,4%. The Y-axis shows values from 0 to 100%. No other age-groups are highlighted. The data comes from a Pew Research survey conducted February 1 to June 10, 2024.

layers: Each age group (18-29, 30-49, 50-64, 65+) contains 8 horizontal bars, one per platform
The 30-49 age group bars are rendered in vivid, saturated colors (highlighted/selected state)
The 18-29, 50-64, and 65+ age group bars are rendered in faded/muted gray-blue tones
YouTube bars are consistently the longest across all age groups

Each platform has a distinct color per the legend: Facebook=light blue, Instagram=medium blue, X=dark navy, Pinterest=medium-dark blue, Snapchat=light sky blue, YouTube=dark navy/teal, Reddit=light steel blue, TikTok=darkest navy

aria_label: This stacked bar chart shows the percentage of U.S. adults who say they selected social media platforms, broken down by age group, based on a Pew Research survey conducted February 1 to June 10, 2024. The eight platforms shown on are Facebook (28.4%), Instagram (33.5%), X (29.1%), Pinterest (30.5%), Snapchat (27.8%), YouTube (27.8%), Reddit (36.5%), and TikTok (29.6%), with the 30-49 age group highlighted in dark blue, the rest of the age groups are filtered out. The Y-axis shows a range from 0 to 100%.

layers: Six box plots displayed side by side, one per political bloc. The Centre box plot is highlighted in yellow with visible tooltips showing all five-number summary statistics plus fences. Extrême gauche is colored red/pink, Gauche in light pink, Centre in yellow/gold, Divers in light grey, Droite in blue, and Extrême droite in dark grey. Outlier points are shown as individual dots or triangles beyond the whiskers for several categories.

filters.label: X-axis categories
filters.value: Extrême gauche, Gauche, Centre, Divers, Droite, Extrême droite

aria_label: This is a box plot showing the distribution of abstention rates at the second round of elections (Taux d'abstention au 2nd tour, in percent) grouped by winning political bloc (Bloc politique vainqueur). Six categories are shown: Extrême gauche, Gauche, Centre, Divers, Droite, and Extrême droite. Outliers for each category are: Extrême gauche - Roubaix and Creil, Gauche - Stains and Lumbres, Centre - L'Isle-d'Abeau and Die, Divers - Rumilly and Sartène, Droite - Tourcoing and Luc-la-Primaube, Extrême droite - Val de Briey and  Canohès. The Centre bloc is highlighted with detailed statistics: minimum 22,43%, lower fence 28,64%, Q1 37,095%, median 41,105%, Q3 44,86%, upper fence 56,35%, and maximum 59,52%. The Y-axis shows range from 10% to 60%

chart_type: Dashboard (composite: KPI cards, data table, combo bar/line chart, choropleth map, gauge chart)

layers.combo_chart: Bar chart (orange/amber fill) showing annual tourist spending in M€ per French region, with a line chart overlay (dark gold) showing GDP share percentage on a secondary right Y-axis. Île-de-France bar is tallest (~40K), Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes second (~22K). Bars fade lighter for smaller regions.
layers.table: Août row is highlighted in dark orange/brown as the peak month. Highlighted column 2023 appears in orange.
layers.map: Choropleth/point map showing tourist itineraries concentrated in the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region with dense orange point clusters.
layers.gauge: Semi-circular gauge filled ~65% in amber/gold showing score 0.65 out of 1.00, with remaining arc in light grey.

aria_label: This is a tourism dashboard for the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region of France. Key performance indicators show 14.6 million arrivals in 2024 (up 2,3% in 2024 versus 2023), 8.81 overnight stays per inhabitant (in 2024), 604.83 arrivals per km² (in 2024), and 2.595K tourist itineraries, with a tourism score of 0.65 out of 1.00. A seasonal table of overnight stays by month shows August as the peak month across all years (reaching 2,793,000 in 2022), while a combo chart compares annual tourist spending in M€ across French regions with Île-de-France leading with a significant margin and Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes second, alongside a line showing each region's share of GDP, with the right Y-axis ranging from 0 to 15%. There is a map of the region showing points of interest for different touristic itineraries.

layers: Each subject has a distinct colored box plot: Maths in pink, Français in light pink, Histoire in yellow, Sciences in light gray, Arts in blue, Sport in dark gray
A single outlier point labeled 'Lucas' is shown in Maths at approximately 20/20
Median lines are drawn across each box in a darker shade of the box color
Whiskers extend from the boxes to minimum and maximum non-outlier values
filters: Legend marker for outlier: 'Lucas' (pink dot)
Six subject categories on X-axis: Maths, Français, Histoire, Sciences, Arts, Sport
aria_label: Box plot titled 'Résultats scolaires par matière - Classe 4A (avec valeurs extrêmes)' showing grade distributions for six subjects in class 4A. The Y-axis labeled 'Notes sur 20' has a range 0 to 20 and the X-axis shows the subjects: Maths, Français, Histoire, Sciences, Arts, Sport. Arts shows the highest median with a narrow interquartile range, while Sciences shows the lowest median. Histoire has the widest spread. A notable outlier labeled 'Lucas' appears in Maths at 20/20. No tooltips are present.

filters: No filters or legend pills visible; six subject categories displayed along X-axis
aria_label: This is a box plot titled 'Résultats scolaires par matière - Classe 2E (dispersion variable)', showing the distribution of grades out of 20 for six subjects in class 2E. Y-axis has a range from 0 to 20. Sport has the highest scores and narrow interquartile range, while Sciences also shows high but very tightly clustered results. Maths and Arts show the greatest variability, with whiskers spanning nearly the full 0–20 range, and Histoire also displays wide dispersion. Français shows the most compact distribution, with tightly grouped scores. No tooltip present.

layers: Each hexagon is filled with a solid color corresponding to the winning political bloc: pink/magenta (Gauche) for Zones A, D, K; blue (Droite) for Zones B, G, J; black (Extrême droite) for Zones C, L; gold/yellow (Centre) for Zones E, F; dark red (Extrême gauche) for Zone H; grey (Divers) for Zone I. No fading or transparency effects applied.

aria_label: This hexagonal tile map titled 'Résultats électoraux par zone — Bloc politique vainqueur' shows the winning political bloc for 12 electoral zones labeled A through L. The Droite (blue) wins the most zones with Zones B, G, and J; Gauche (pink) wins Zones A, D, and K; Extrême droite (black) wins Zones C and L; Centre (gold) wins Zones E and F; while Extrême gauche (dark red) wins Zone H and Divers (grey) wins Zone I. There is a legend bottom right on the chart with all the political blocs listed. No tooltip is visible and nothing is highlighted.

aria_label: This is a hexagonal tile map titled 'Prix médian au m² par zone (gradient de couleur)' showing the median price per square meter by zone using a blue color gradient. The color scale ranges from 800 € (lightest blue) to 7,200 € (darkest blue). Zone C and Zone J stand out as the most expensive zones, displayed in the darkest blue near 7,200 €/m², while Zone A, Zone D, Zone H, and Zone K appear in the lightest shades indicating the lowest prices closest to 800 €/m². A color scale legend on the right shows values from 800 € (lightest blue) to 7,200 € (darkest blue), with 4,000 € at the midpoint. No tooltip is visible and nothing is highlighted.
